div.head: 353. Editorial Note
div.p: Prime Minister Pipinelis resigned on September 26 following protests by the Center Union that his links with ERE and the repeated election law violations during the 1961 campaign necessitated a neutral figure at the head of the Greek Government during national elections. King Paul asked the President of the Supreme Court, Stylianos Mavromichalies, to form a government to oversee elections. Mavromichalies presented his government to Parliament on September 28 and announced that the King would call new elections for November 3.
